---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

```

## YouTube Video Watching Habits

This report summarizes responses collected from a survey about YouTube video watching habits.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

logged_data <- read.csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vT6Sj-mk7hV4dVcJp4qxMJ4wSonNCLUMbO0Eng6lbrZIXQa_HT-SECvNb0g_WnVI5HnbATr69Yt_JCJ/pub?output=csv")

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    timestamp = Timestamp,
    video_length = Question.1..How.long.was.the.video.,
    video_type = Question.2..What.type.of.video.was.it.,
    device = Question.3..What.device.did.you.watch.on.,
    watch_time = Question.4..What.time.of.day.did.you.watch.it.
  )

mean_length <- mean(latest_data$video_length, na.rm = TRUE)
max_length <- max(latest_data$video_length, na.rm = TRUE)
```

The average video length recorded was `r round(mean_length, 1)` minutes,  
with the longest video being `r max_length` minutes.

```{r}
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = video_type)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Video Type Distribution",
       x = "Video Type",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = device)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "tomato") +
  labs(title = "Device Used to Watch Videos",
       x = "Device",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```
